rule: normalization statistics fit only on train prefix or train units
online rows must emit prediction/action before target/reward update
future targets are shifted before chronological splitting
all standardized streams must be finite before scoring; non-finite rows block the run
all thresholds, policy costs, lags, reservoirs, and hyperparameters locked before test scoring
candidate and baselines are compared on the same seeds and streams
baselines receive equivalent causal features or any advantage is explicitly labeled
oracle is reported as upper bound only and never treated as a beatable baseline
C-MAPSS/NAB signals cannot erase a failed standardized core scoreboard
